Seasons Greetings my little droogie,
It's the vear end of the 50th r,ear annir.ersary of one of my favorite all-time

Specificall1, Gene's "5th stage headline." One of Gene's great genius insights was the idea
of market awareness. How aware is your market of the problem your product solves?
When your market is in the 5th stage of its awareness, that is when you write a headline
that is all about the market - flags them down - that sells nothing promises nothing,
and satisfies nothing. It is simply "echoing an emotion." And you know what? I have
found this type of headline to be the perfect kind of subject line and theme lor emails
long term - doing daily broadcast emails, Iong auto-responder sequences, and, yes, even
afEliate offers. I have been thinking about this off and on for years. But if there's one
thing that has allowed me to bang out emails that consistently get read and bought from
in multiple markets, over long periods of time, even when I'm selling mostly the same
thing day in, and day out, without mass numbers of people getting bored or optine out...
it is I write what I call "5th stage emails." Meaning, most of my emails don't look like I'm

I\otice this is a 5th stage email through and through. It starts out haüng nothing at
all to do with becoming an expert, MLM, making money, or any of that. It simply taps

dudes worry about as Western society continues its moral descent into chaos - with

(starting with the subject line - talking about avery real insecurity American men

because, again, that was the email with the highest clicks and sales (and we had a

This is true. This is also why this silly idea of trying to position yourself as a great
,,teacher" before being seen as a great "entertainer" is a huge mistake - the whining of

second. It is something I've done for going on 10 years since I first heard the great Matt

me? Then think oi á. hieJohnny Carson.Johnny was the host of The Tonight Shout
for 30 years. He was also the ONE guy that even all the A-list Hollywood actors,

parry .rre, th. AJistiest of A-listers would seekJohnny out. They would ask the host
i,*há, isJohnny coming?" They would wait forJohnny and the even the Academy
Awards glt him to host their show for 5 years. Why? Because he was the greatest

as you could g.i. Ara he had arguably more influence-over people thananyone at the
tiáe. The pro=d,r..r, of his show have even admitted thatJohnny - had he taken

l could have easily swayed elections.Just one well-placed joke at the right time and
BAM! He could have ináuenced the votes of millions - maybe even tens of millions -
of people with one or two sentences.
That's powet baby.
The kind of power that comes only from being a high functioning entertainer'
And so, when writing your emails never be afraid to entertain. As Matt Fury
teaches, iew. People crave
entertainment. Ttrey crave wanting to feel good. They crave haüng someone take their
inds off their troubles and, at the same time in email, give value. This is the essence ofITIlNOS

The Power Of Bragging
About Your Product's Flaws
If you've been orbiting Settle World for very long, you've no doubt heard Yours
Unruly talk about the concept of "making the skeleton dance." This is the sales
philosophy of sales trainer and author Barry Maher. He said it is based on something
George Bernard Shaw said, "If you cant't hide thefatnity skeleton, you rnight as
ruell tnake it dance. " In selling and marketing that simply means finding your product's
flaws, problems, etc and, instead of hiding them in the closet hoping nobody will notice
them or call them out... you BRAG about them instead.
Okay, so I've talked about this many times.
But, what I want to do today is show you actual examples of how this works. It's
one thing for me to tell you about this concept. But it's quite another to show you how it
worls in the real world.Just realize it could take you some time and eflort to implement
this and turn your negatives into reasons to buy. But, once you do, you're emails, sales
letters, and anything else you market with are going to take your response and sales to a
whole new level.

THE. EMAIL PLAYERS NAWSLETTER
Making The Skeleton Dance Exarnple #l
American Giant has an $89 sweatshirt that has a six month wait to get it. Do they
hide this fact? I\o, they BRAG about it. You can see it in their ads and even when
reporters write about it üey go out of their way to say how long the wait is and why.
Their reason? Because they are so popular (i.e. social proof). It's a brilliant use of making
the skeleton dance.
Making The Skeleton Dance Exarnple #2
Another example in the same industry (apparel)would be my friend Pete Van Zijl.
He sells high quality apparel to people who are in the military military vets, policemen,
firefighters, etc. I talked to him about his business once and he was saying how a big
complaint his customers had was the long wait. \Mhy would they want to buy his apparel
which takes weeks to reach them when they could just go on Amazon and get something
similar within two days? My answer? "Make that skeleton break dance!" I told him to
brag in his advertising and emails about how long it takes because they use nothing but
the best quality material that is harder to get, that they inspect ever¡hing with extra care
before it leaves the factory and refuse to sell apparel that's going to whither away in a few
months after several washing machine cycles. The best quality always takes longer and is
more expensive. And so on, and so forth.
Making The Skeleton Dance Exarnple #3
If you ever see the old Aüs car ads they did this brilliantly. I was just re-reading
Gene Schwartz's BreaktlroughAduertising book and came across one of their ads. It
says, to'We're nurnber 2. Therefore we try harder." Bam. One hundred percent
transparency, and taking their negative (we're not the biggest or maybe even the best by
implication)... but because of that we are going the exúa mile (so to speak). It "says" a
great deat without haüng to say it. They are essentially bragging about being #2.
Making The Slreleton Dance Exarnple #4
This was an example Barry gave in an interview he did with Michael Senof! "Are
our machines more expensive? Absolutely. Are they Iess reliable? Absolutely. But, we're
the leaders in the marketplace. Why do you think that happens in spite of the fact that
we're more expensive, in spite of the fact that we're less reliable?" And then, he goes into

the benefits.
Making The Skeleton Dance Exarnple #5
Here's another example straight from the Maher's mouth (talking about what he
tells clients who wince at his high fees), 'Are my fees expensive? Absolutely. Why do I
charge so much? Because I can - because my clients aren't just willing to pay these kinds
of rates, they're hrppy to pay them for the kind of results I generate. I'm thinking of
raising them." Pure brilliance.
Making The Skeleton Dance Exarnple #6
One more Barry Maher example coming right out and telling his customer he
makes a commission on the sale ("pply this to affiliate marketing - I do, albeit with
different words), "I don't want you to forget I'm on commission here. The more you
spend the more I make. No*, let me tell you why you need to be spending more and
making me more money." And üen, goes on to tell the benefits.
Ary*uy, go ye forth and make the skeleton dance.
It'll be one of the single most profitable things you can ever do in your emails.

Tuu EMAIL PLAYERS NewSLETTER
Later, check those links out for several more and how Grry turned them into sales
letter openers. For now, I want to give you a huge list of unusual facts you can use for your
daily emails (which I believe this works even better for - as it constantly keeps your list
on their toes and prevents them from becoming easily bored). I got the fotlowing as one of
those annoyng email forward chains from my own mother. And, I have been going
through it a lot lately fishing email ideas out for various products. I have no idea who
compiled it. And, I have no idea if they are accurate for sure, but here they is. I
recommend making it a goal to write at least one email from this list. Then, next week, do
the same, then another one the week after that. Not only will it make for some great
emails, but will force your brain to connect ideas and concepts and facts into eÁails you
can use to sell stuff with.
Here goes:
Money isn't rnade out of paper, it's rnade out of cotton.
The dot over the letter i is called a "tittLe"
A raisin dropped in a glass of fresh charnpagne will bounce up and
down continuously frorn the bottorn of the glass to the top.
315 entries in Webster's 1996 Dictionary were misspelled.
